title: Plugin chain P6: MCP task-store server (the broaden-to-non-Code seam)
description:
Sixth ticket of the plugin packaging chain (epic TASK-020). Per `tasks/TASK-020.research.md` §E. This is the 'broaden later' seam — it branches off P3 and is the one piece deferrable without blocking the core 'install and develop' experience for Claude Code devs. Implement an MCP server bundled in the plugin that exposes the task store as tools, so a non-Claude-Code client (claude.ai/Desktop) can read and mutate the backlog.

Scope:
1. `src/mcp-server.js` exposing six tools (thin wrappers over existing `src/task-store.js`): `list_todos`, `list_ready`, `get_task`, `create_task`, `transition_status`, `append_comment` (full arg/return contract in §E.1). Each binds `repoRoot` to the project root the server was launched against (`${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}`).
2. `.mcp.json` at the plugin root registering the server via `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/src/mcp-server.js` with `NODE_PATH` from P3's data-dir.
3. Tool names mirror the Jira-compatible field names already in `tasks/schema.json`, so the surface survives the eventual Atlassian-MCP migration (backend swaps; tool names stay).

**Scope boundary:** this exposes the ticket store only. It explicitly does NOT attempt to port the orchestrator/subagent loop or RESUME-FIRST orchestration — those are Claude Code-exclusive (§E.2). Document the WILL/WON'T for non-Code clients.
acceptance_criteria: `src/mcp-server.js` exposes list_todos, list_ready, get_task, create_task, transition_status, append_comment, each wrapping the corresponding task-store.js function and binding repoRoot to ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}.
`.mcp.json` at the plugin root registers the server; after install `claude plugin details` shows MCP servers (1) with no startup errors.
A test round-trips through the tools against a temp-project task store: create_task → list_todos shows it → transition_status to done → get_task reflects the new status.
The server's docstring/README states the explicit WILL/WON'T for a non-Claude-Code client (gets ticket CRUD; does NOT get the orchestrator/subagent loop or RESUME-FIRST orchestration).

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Minted from TASK-020 spike §F (P6) + §E. Medium priority — branches off P3 and is deferrable per the 'devs now, broaden later' direction. Rejoins the chain at P7's E2E. Tool surface intentionally mirrors Jira field names for loss-free TASK-003 migration later.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: CLOSED APPROVE (auto-close per standing plugin-chain authorization — cleanest review of the whole chain: 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 0 LOW / 1 NIT). Researcher first produced the reusable mcp-server skill (.claude/skills/mcp-server/ + plugin-root mirror) + the new KB entry proposal; tests-first bb1a663; impl 2447977. Reviewer (fresh context): full suite 339 passed / 4 skipped DETERMINISTIC across two clean runs, `claude plugin validate .` PASS, dist/mcp-server.cjs == fresh build. ALL 4 ACs MET. Delivered: src/mcp-server.js exposes a createServer({repoRoot}) factory + main() registering the six tools (list_todos/list_ready/get_task/create_task/transition_status/append_comment) via McpServer.registerTool with raw-zod-shape inputSchemas, each binding repoRoot (factory arg, or process.env.CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR ?? process.cwd() in main); five wrap src/task-store.js, get_task implemented inline (no store export). Results are {content:[{type:'text',text:JSON.stringify(...)}]}; errors throw → SDK isError. Repo-root .mcp.json (stdio: node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/mcp-server.cjs, env CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR); plugin.json re-declares mcpServers './.mcp.json' (the key P7 removed — file now exists so the publish-config guard + flipped plugin-scaffold assertion pass); third esbuild entry → committed dist/mcp-server.cjs; shipped-bin.json updated. @modelcontextprotocol/sdk@^1.29.0 + zod@^3.25.0 as devDeps (esbuild-inlined, no runtime deps). AC3 round-trips through a REAL SDK Client over InMemoryTransport.createLinkedPair() (create→list→transition→get + done-task drops from list_todos). SECURITY (reviewer-scrutinized, SOUND): get_task's /^TASK-\d{3,}$/ guard runs BEFORE any path join/read — rejects ../../etc/passwd, TASK-1/../../x, newline-injection; well-formed-absent key → null via ENOENT; other tools never build paths from raw input. JSON-RPC SAFETY (sound): zero stdout writes, only console.error to stderr. WILL/WON'T docstring present (AC4). NIT (a redundant McpServer re-import in the test) deferred. NET NEW TESTS this ticket. PLUGIN CHAIN (epic TASK-020) NOW 100% COMPLETE — all 7 tickets (P1-P7) closed & reviewer-APPROVED. Remaining operational follow-up (NOT code, NOT blocking): the live `claude plugin install` → `claude plugin details` MCP-servers(1) sensor + the TASK-027 AC1 live agent-loop E2E — both inherently manual, run at install time.
